have identical dimensions.

|     |     |     | 0   |     |     | 0   |     |     |

|     |     |     | 5   |     |     | 5   |     |     |
|     |     |     | 6   |     |     | 6   |     |     |
X
Y

Which statement about X and Y is correct?

options.A: They have different volumes and different weights.
options.B: They have different volumes and equal weights.
options.C: They have equal volumes and equal weights.
options.D: They have equal volumes and different weights.

options.A: the momentum of a system is conserved in all interactions
options.C: force acting = change of momentum  time

| A  the momentum of a system is conserved in all interactions  |     |     |     |     |     |     |

10  A hot gas and a cool liquid are at the same pressure.

Which row describes the separation and the motion of the particles of a hot gas compared to
those of a cool liquid?

|     | separation of gas particles  |     | motion of gas particles    |     |     |
| --- | ---------------------------- | --- | -------------------------- | --- | --- |
| A   | greater than a cool liquid   |     | faster than a cool liquid  |     |     |
| B   | greater than a cool liquid   |     | slower than a cool liquid  |     |     |
| C   | less than a cool liquid      |     | faster than a cool liquid  |     |     |
| D   | less than a cool liquid      |     | slower than a cool liquid  |     |     |

items.stem:
The specific heat capacity of solid P is greater than that of solid Q.
What does this statement mean?

options.A: Less energy is needed to raise the temperature by 1°C of unit mass of solid P than unit mass of solid Q.
options.B: Less energy is needed to melt unit mass of solid P than unit mass of solid Q.
options.C: More energy is needed to raise the temperature by 1°C of unit mass of solid P than unit mass of solid Q.
options.D: More energy is needed to melt unit mass of solid P than unit mass of solid Q.

items.skill: Interpret specific heat capacity definition

raw.normalized_raw_block:
12 The specific heat capacity of solid P is greater than that of solid Q.
What does this statement mean?
A Less energy is needed to raise the temperature by 1°C of unit mass of solid P than unit mass
of solid Q.
B Less energy is needed to melt unit mass of solid P than unit mass of solid Q.
C More energy is needed to raise the temperature by 1°C of unit mass of solid P than unit
mass of solid Q.
D More energy is needed to melt unit mass of solid P than unit mass of solid Q.

options.B: The forces of attraction between the gas particles and the walls of the container increase.
options.C: The gas particles collide with each other more frequently.
options.D: The gas particles lose more energy when they strike the walls of the container.

Which statement explains this increase in pressure?
A The forces of the gas particles striking the walls of the container increase.
B The forces of attraction between the gas particles and the walls of the container increase.
C The gas particles collide with each other more frequently.
D The gas particles lose more energy when they strike the walls of the container.

options.A: The copper rod feels colder than the wooden rod because copper is a better conductor than wood.
options.B: The copper rod feels warmer than the wooden rod because copper is a better conductor than wood.
options.C: The wooden rod feels colder than the copper rod because wood is a better conductor than copper.
options.D: The wooden rod feels warmer than the copper rod because wood is a better conductor than copper.

items.skill: Compare thermal conduction of materials

issues: stem is empty — check raw_block for correct stem boundary
option D has trailing page number — stripped in normalized output

raw.normalized_raw_block:
15 A student has a copper rod and a wooden rod. Both rods have the same dimensions.
The student puts one end of each rod into a beaker of crushed ice.
After a few minutes, the student picks up the rods by the ends not in the ice.
Which statement is correct?
A The copper rod feels colder than the wooden rod because copper is a better conductor than
wood.
B The copper rod feels warmer than the wooden rod because copper is a better conductor than
wood.
C The wooden rod feels colder than the copper rod because wood is a better conductor than
copper.
D The wooden rod feels warmer than the copper rod because wood is a better conductor than
copper.
raw.original_raw_block:

A The copper rod feels colder than the wooden rod because copper is a better conductor than
wood.
B The copper rod feels warmer than the wooden rod because copper is a better conductor than
wood.
C The wooden rod feels colder than the copper rod because wood is a better conductor than
copper.
D The wooden rod feels warmer than the copper rod because wood is a better conductor than
copper.

The diagram shows a black object X at constant temperature.

The arrows represent the absorption and emission of thermal radiation at the surface.

Object Y is at the same constant temperature as X and is identical except that it has a white
surface.

|     | rate of  | rate of  |     |

| absorption for X  |         | emission for X  |     |
| ----------------- | ------- | --------------- | --- |

items.skill: Compare speed and wavelength of sound in different media

issues: stem is empty — check raw_block for correct stem boundary
has markdown table — options may be partial
has diagram/visual content — options may be labels only

raw.normalized_raw_block:
23  A sound wave travels through the air and strikes a solid block of steel.

Some of the sound is reflected from the steel, and some is transmitted through the steel.

How does the speed and the wavelength of the sound in steel compare to the speed and the
wavelength of the sound in air?

|     | speed in steel       |     | wavelength in steel  |     |     |
| --- | -------------------- | --- | -------------------- | --- | --- |
| A   | less than in air     |     | less than in air     |     |     |
| B   | less than in air     |     | greater than in air  |     |     |
| C   | greater than in air  |     | less than in air     |     |     |
| D   | greater than in air  |     | greater than in air  |     |     |

|     | description of electric field  |     |     | direction of electric field  |     |     |     |
| --- | ------------------------------ | --- | --- | ---------------------------- | --- | --- | --- |
| A   | region in which an electric    |     |     | direction of the force       |     |     |     |
|     | charge experiences a force     |     |     | on a positive charge         |     |     |     |
| B   | region in which an electric    |     |     | direction of the force       |     |     |     |
|     | charge experiences a force     |     |     | on a negative charge         |     |     |     |

|     | charge produces a current      |     |     | on a positive charge         |     |     |     |
| D   | region in which an electric    |     |     | direction of the force       |     |     |     |
|     | charge produces a current      |     |     | on a negative charge         |     |     |     |

items.stem:
Copper wire is available in fixed lengths but in various diameters d. Each wire has a different
resistance R.

Which relationship between R and d is correct?

options.A: R is directly proportional to d.
options.B: R is directly proportional to d².
options.C: R is inversely proportional to d.
options.D: R is inversely proportional to d².

items.skill: Determine resistance–diameter relationship

issues: Missing options: ['D']
has markdown table — options may be partial
has diagram/visual content — options may be labels only
correct answer 'D' not found in parsed options
options reconciled from split markdown table

raw.normalized_raw_block:
26  Copper wire is available in fixed lengths but in various diameters d. Each wire has a different
resistance R.

Which relationship between R and d is correct?
| A   | R is directly proportional to d.   |     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | ---------------------------------- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| B   | R is directly proportional to d2.  |     |     |     |     |     |     |

| C   | R is inversely proportional to d.  |     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | ---------------------------------- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
R is inversely proportional to d2.
D

What is the function of the iron core in the transformer?
A to conduct current from the primary coil to the secondary coil

C to ensure there is a high voltage between the primary coil and the secondary coil
D to link the magnetic field in the primary coil to the secondary coil

issues: stem is empty — check raw_block for correct stem boundary

raw.normalized_raw_block:
35 A detector placed near a radioactive isotope gives a measured count rate of 800 counts/minute.
The half-life of the isotope is three hours.
The background count rate is 32 counts/minute.
What is the count rate on the detector after six hours?
A 192 counts/minute
B 200 counts/minute
C 224 counts/minute
D 232 counts/minute

items.stem:
Sodium-24 is a radioactive isotope that emits beta radiation.
Which nuclear equation shows how sodium-24 decays?
items.skill: Interpret nuclear decay equation

issues: option D has trailing page number — stripped in normalized output
raw block contains (cid:) characters — symbol extraction may be incorrect

raw.normalized_raw_block:
36 Sodium-24 is a radioactive isotope that emits beta radiation.
Which nuclear equation shows how sodium-24 decays?
A 24Na -> 24Mg + 0beta
11 12 –1
B 24Na -> 24Ne + 0beta
11 10 –1
C 24Na -> 20F + 4beta
11 9 2
D 24Na -> 20Al + 4beta
11 13 2
raw.original_raw_block:
36 Sodium-24 is a radioactive isotope that emits beta radiation.
Which nuclear equation shows how sodium-24 decays?
A 24Na (cid:1) 24Mg + 0(cid:2)
11 12 –1
B 24Na (cid:1) 24Ne + 0(cid:2)
11 10 –1
C 24Na (cid:1) 20F + 4(cid:2)
11 9 2
D 24Na (cid:1) 20Al + 4(cid:2)
11 13 2

items.stem:
Which safety precautions must be taken when using a source of gamma radiation?
1 Reduce the distance between the source and the person.
2 Reduce the time of exposure to the radiation.
3 Use a suitable shielding material between the source and the person.
items.skill: Recall gamma radiation safety precautions

1 Reduce the distance between the source and the person.

A 1, 2 and 3 B 1 and 2 only C 1 and 3 only D 2 and 3 only

items.stem: Which statement about the Universe is correct?

options.B: Redshift is a decrease in the observed wavelength of electromagnetic radiation emitted from receding galaxies.
options.C: Light emitted from distant galaxies appears redshifted.
options.D: Redshift of the light from distant galaxies gives evidence that the Universe is contracting.

items.skill: Recall properties of the Universe and redshift

raw.normalized_raw_block:
39  Which statement about the Universe is correct?
A  The Universe consists of 100000 galaxies.

B  Redshift is a decrease in the observed wavelength of electromagnetic radiation emitted from
receding galaxies.
C  Light emitted from distant galaxies appears redshifted.
D  Redshift of the light from distant galaxies gives evidence that the Universe is contracting.
raw.original_raw_block:
39  Which statement about the Universe is correct?
A  The Universe consists of 100000 galaxies.

B  Redshift is a decrease in the observed wavelength of electromagnetic radiation emitted from
receding galaxies.
C  Light emitted from distant galaxies appears redshifted.
D  Redshift of the light from distant galaxies gives evidence that the Universe is contracting.

items.stem: Which statement about the cosmic microwave background radiation (CMBR) is correct?

options.A: CMBR now has a frequency higher than that of infrared radiation.
options.B: CMBR has always had exactly the same frequency.
options.C: CMBR had a shorter wavelength in the past.
options.D: CMBR was produced a long time after the Universe was formed.
